LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEC-21-2021 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF ERIC NANS'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND DOCUMENTS; AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION. THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS LARGELY ADOPTED AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ERIC THRONE, CSR NO. 7855, EMAIL: ERIC.THRONE.CSR@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for oral argument on Plaintiff's request for sanctions.

Dept. 505; Judge Harry Jacobs; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 02:32:18 p.m. and concluding at 02:56:55 p.m.

Plaintiff Eric Nans repreented by Christopher R. Leclerc, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Video Tech Services, Inc. represented by Louis Kosnett, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.

The matter is submitted.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. OLEG I. ALBERT, Esq. of Le Clerc & Le Clerc, LLP (Tel: 415-445-0900) for plaintiff Eric Nans;
Atty. LOUIS V. KOSNETT, Esq. of Kosnett Law Firm (Tel: 310-445-5900) for defendants Video Tech Services, Inc. and John Scott Manzano.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ERIC NANS's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents; And Request For Sanctions.

Matter is argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted with the exception of the following sentence: "The JPT further recommends that the parties split the cost of sending a neutral copy service over to copy and scan the documents." Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling Defendant to provide further responses to special interrogatories and requests for documents is granted in part and denied in part as set forth in the recommendation. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ERIC NANS's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents; And Request For Sanctions.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Court has not received electronic courtesy copies of the Opposition or the Reply papers. The motion is continued to December 1, 2021 for counsel to provide electronic courtesy copies of the Opposition and Reply papers. Per the instructions posted on the department's website (https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/civil/law-motion), parties must attach electronic copies of discovery motion papers to an email addressed to Discovery302@sftc.org. The subject line of the email should state the case name and number and the continued hearing date.
